description: Custom Persona creation enhancement + API Token authentication + Docker/deploy improvements
steps: Step 1 - Custom Persona Enhancement: In dashboard/templates/create_persona.html, add support for editing and deleting existing custom personas. Add a list of existing custom personas at the top of the page (fetched from GET /api/custom-personas). Each item has Edit and Delete buttons.

Step 2 - Custom Persona API: In dashboard/app.py, add GET /api/custom-personas (list all custom personas from personas/custom/ directory), DELETE /api/custom-persona/{agent_id} (delete the YAML file and unregister from registry), PUT /api/custom-persona/{agent_id} (update existing persona YAML).

Step 3 - Custom Persona Badge: In dashboard/templates/personas.html, add a 'Custom' badge (styled distinctly in orange/purple) next to personas loaded from the custom/ directory. The personas page already renders all registered personas - add a field 'is_custom' to the persona meta returned by _persona_meta() in app.py to identify custom ones.

Step 4 - API Token Auth: In dashboard/app.py, add a middleware that checks for AUGUR_API_TOKEN environment variable. When set, all /api/* requests must include 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' header. When not set (default), API remains fully open. Return 401 JSON error if token is invalid/missing.
Step 5 - API Token Verify: Add endpoint GET /api/auth/verify that returns {status: 'ok', authenticated: true} if token is valid, or 401 if not. This allows the frontend to check token validity.

Step 6 - Frontend Token: In dashboard/static/js/i18n.js (or a new utils section in base.html), patch the global fetchWithTimeout function to automatically include the Authorization header if an API token is stored in localStorage under key 'augur_api_token'. In dashboard/templates/settings.html, add an 'API Token' configuration section where users can paste their token (stored to localStorage).
Step 7 - .env.example update: Add AUGUR_API_TOKEN to .env.example with a comment explaining it's optional.

Step 8 - Docker: Verify Dockerfile works correctly. Ensure docker-compose.yml passes AUGUR_API_TOKEN as an environment variable to the dashboard service. Add the AUGUR_API_TOKEN environment variable declaration.
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/custom-personas returns a list of custom personas
Custom personas show a distinct 'Custom' badge on /personas page
When AUGUR_API_TOKEN env is set, /api/* endpoints return 401 without proper Bearer token
When AUGUR_API_TOKEN is NOT set, all APIs work without authentication (backward compatible)
Settings page has API Token input field that saves to localStorage
fetchWithTimeout auto-includes Authorization header when token is in localStorage
verification: python -c "import os; os.environ['AUGUR_API_TOKEN']='test123'; from fastapi.testclient import TestClient; from dashboard.app import app; c = TestClient(app); r = c.get('/api/personas'); print('401 without token:', r.status_code == 401); r2 = c.get('/api/auth/verify', headers={'Authorization': 'Bearer test123'}); print('verify:', r2.status_code, r2.json())"
